title: CASE 1.1 standards-alignment for >= 1 open framework
context: CASE (Competencies and Academic Standards Exchange) 1.1 is the 1EdTech standard for machine-readable curriculum alignment. Adding standards alignment metadata to banks lets teachers filter and find items by standard — but only for openly licensed frameworks where redistribution of identifiers/derivatives is permitted. The framework license must be verified before embedding any standard text; restricted standard text may be referenced but not reproduced. Alignment is optional metadata; its absence must never block a bank.
objective: Implement CASE 1.1 standards-alignment support in the canonical model's alignment.standardId field for at least one openly licensed framework (e.g. Common Core), with the framework license verified.
acceptanceCriteria: The chosen framework's license is verified to permit redistribution of identifiers/derivatives (e.g. Common Core CC-BY-style with attribution); restricted frameworks are reference-only or omitted.
Implements CASE 1.1 alignment in alignment.standardId; alignment is optional metadata and its absence never blocks a bank from shipping.
No restricted standard text is reproduced where the license forbids it; restricted frameworks are referenced by identifier only.
Golden fixtures and CI tests cover both aligned and non-aligned items.
Code is MIT-licensed; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; commits DCO signed-off.
resources: Common Core State Standards license (CC-BY-style with attribution/branding conditions)
output: CASE 1.1 standards-alignment implementation in the canonical tooling (MIT-licensed) for at least one license-verified open framework, with CI tests and golden fixtures.
